channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Contractor assessed its own service in ICB procurement
item.description: An integrated care board has been criticised by a watchdog over poor record-keeping and errors in the decision to award a £2.4m contract.

item.title: Dispute flares up over health centre development
item.description: An integrated care board is in dispute with one of its primary care networks over a bid to convert a former police station into a health centre.

item.title: New maternity inspection ‘unit’ demanded by government review
item.description: Ministers should create a new specialist unit to assess maternity services because the Care Quality Commission does not have the credibility to do so, a government review has concluded.

item.title: Resident doctors accept pay offer
item.description: Resident doctors in England have voted to accept an offer from the government following long-running industrial action.

item.title: Exclusive: Maternity adviser quits in ‘normal birth’ dispute
item.description: The chair of several high-profile safety inquiries has resigned from the government’s national maternity review just ahead of publication, in a dispute over “normal birth ideology”, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: ICB boss to lead regional team
item.description: An NHS England regional director is to step down after just over a year in the role, HSJ has learned.

item.title: Trust takes £2m hit on flawed land sale
item.description: A trust was forced to pay £2m to exit a land sale – after realising that it might need the space and would have lost money on the deal.

item.title: Exclusive: Regulators poised to strip back AI rules
item.description: Regulators are about to significantly strip back regulation of ambient voice technology – one of the fastest-growing healthcare AI tools – HSJ has learned.

item.title: NHS manager died after being ‘lost to follow up’
item.description: An NHS manager died after an urgent referral was “recategorised” and a triage time of six weeks was arranged instead.

item.title: Revealed: Promised ‘neighbourhood health centres’ already met requirements
item.description: Nearly all the first wave of “neighbourhood health centres” – currently being developed for launch by next year – were already doing the job required of the model, government documents reveal.

item.title: Ex-NHSE strategy director joins major PR firm
item.description: Former NHS England executive board member Chris Hopson has been appointed to a senior advisory role at a major communications consultancy.

item.title: Heatwave shuts theatres and scanners
item.description: Theatres were shut and equipment broke down as the heatwave caused widespread disruption across the NHS’s ageing estate.

item.title: Exclusive: Another major trust sacks staff over snooping
item.description: Members of staff from yet another NHS trust have been sacked for inappropriately viewing patient medical records, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Behave properly or leave, trust tells staff
item.description: A trust grappling with cultural challenges has told staff it is “drawing a line in the sand” on “disrespectful, dismissive, or undermining behaviour”.

item.title: Families sue over care failures
item.description: More than a dozen families have taken legal action against the NHS over care failings in paediatric audiology services, HSJ has discovered.

item.title: Mackey: We’ll change exec contracts to ensure accountability for failings
item.description: NHS executives could have their contracts rewritten to ensure they can be held to account for any actions taken while working for previous employers, NHS England’s CEO has said.

item.title: Behaviour concerns ‘swept under carpet’ at struggling trust
item.description: One of the NHS’s worst-performing trusts was beset by poor behaviour “at all levels”, according to an external review obtained by HSJ.

item.title: Trusts must recheck 10 years’ worth of mortuary records
item.description: Trusts must check records stretching back to 2016 to ensure any failings that have taken place in their mortuaries have been reported to the regulator.

item.title: Snooping on records ‘worrying trend, not just isolated incidents’
item.description: High-profile cases of staff snooping on patients’ health records, as most recently exposed by HSJ, have revealed “a worrying trend” across the NHS rather than just isolated incidences, a watchdog chief has warned.

item.title: Updated: Nottingham families slam ‘abhorrent’ NHS leadership as execs threatened with prison
item.description: Families affected by the Nottingham maternity scandal have hit out at the “abhorrent” leadership of “senior staff” as the government revealed that staff who refuse to engage with future investigations could be sent to prison for up to two years.
